items.title: SSP Celebrates Month of Military Child
items.description: Every April, the Department of War (DoW) celebrates the Month of the Military Child, recognizing the important role military children play in the armed forces community. While the nation’s warfighters are protecting the homeland and her interests around the world, the children of U.S. military servicemembers make sacrifices that effect their daily life.

items.title: JTF-DC Recognizes Month of the Military Child
items.description: April is Month of the Military Child. With more than 1.6 million military children making daily sacrifices, this time is used to recognize their strength, resilience and unique challenges as a result of their parent’s service. April 15th is designated as “Purple Up!” Day. The color purple represents the combined colors of all military branches. Local government, schools, military serving organizations, companies and private citizens are encouraged to wear purple in, support of military children ...

items.title: Lethal Kinetic Strike, April 14, 2026
items.description: On April 14, at the direction of the commander of U.S. Southern Command, Gen. Francis L. Donovan, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations. Intelligence confirmed the vessel was transiting along known narco-trafficking routes in the Eastern Pacific and was engaged in narco-trafficking operations.  Four male narco-terrorists were killed during this action. No U.S. military forces were harmed.

items.title: Coast Guard rescues 4 from vessel trapped in ice near Chefornak, Alaska
items.description: An MH-60 Jayhawk helicopter aircrew from Coast Guard Air Station Kodiak rescues three adults and one child from an 18-foot vessel trapped on an ice floe approximately 10 miles west of Chefornak, Alaska, April 14, 2026. The family of four was on a seal hunting expedition and requested Coast Guard assistance Saturday after being trapped on the ice for over 24 hours and seeing no way out. (U.S. Coast Guard video)

items.title: Small Satellites, Big Impact — MC3 Network Inspires Space Innovation at NPS
items.description: On Jan. 14, 2025, a commercial SpaceX rocket successfully launched from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California, carrying an NPS small satellite, also known as a CubeSat, called Otter. Two hours later, a small team of NPS researchers huddled inside the school’s Satellite Operations Center (SOC), the hub for interacting with NPS assets in space as well as the central node of its globe-spanning Mobile CubeSat Command and Control (MC3) ground network.

items.title: Betting your future: Is gambling a threat to your career and well-being?
items.description: When a service member is struggling, the signs can often point to familiar stressors like anxiety, depression, or sleep problems.
